originalTitle: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
summary: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.
content: An AI-generated anti-drug video from Hong Kong's Correctional Services Department is facing backlash for allegedly making substances look too appealing. The video was released by authorities on 26 June for International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking. The BBC's Chinese service reporter Martin Yip explains how the controversy unfolded.
chineseTitle: 这段 AI 禁毒视频让毒品看起来有魅力吗？
chineseSummary: 香港惩教署删除了一段由人工智能生成的韩国流行音乐视频，此前该视频遭到强烈反对，认为该视频使物质看起来太有吸引力。
chineseContent: 香港惩教署的一段人工智能生成的禁毒视频因涉嫌使物质看起来太有吸引力而面临强烈反对。该视频由当局于 6 月 26 日国际禁止药物滥用和非法贩运日发布。 BBC 中文台记者 Martin Yip 解释了这场争议是如何展开的。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
